pattern: Grouping files by their content string after parsing each directory entry, then keeping only the content buckets holding two or more files, reveals every duplicate group.
hints: Each directory line names a folder followed by files written as name and parenthesized content.
Build the full path for every file and group those paths by their content string.
Any content shared by two or more files forms a duplicate group; discard buckets with a single file.

edge_inputs.description: two duplicate groups across nested directories
same name different directories same content
same content three copies one group

stress.description: random directories and files drawing content from a small pool to force duplicate groups
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 40))
    contents = ['c%d' % i for i in range(5)]
    paths = []
    name_counter = 0
    for d in range(n):
        directory = 'root/dir%d' % d
        nfiles = rng.randint(1, 3)
        files = []
        for _ in range(nfiles):
            name = 'f%d.txt' % name_counter
            name_counter += 1
            content = rng.choice(contents)
            files.append('%s(%s)' % (name, content))
        paths.append(directory + ' ' + ' '.join(files))
    return [paths]
